behavior_summary: This script implements the inspect ('U8P_FIRESHLD::look') description and a hit reaction for the Fire Shield item.
U8P_FIRESHLD::look is a simple process that presents the short label 'fire shield' when the player examines the item.

U8P_FIRESHLD::gotHit is a process invoked when the shield is struck: it identifies the source (NPC vs non‑NPC), computes the direction from shield to source, maps that direction to two numeric parameters (likely velocity/flag values), plays an impact sound, may apply a small randomized 'receive hit' effect, optionally resets a related process reference for the source, then launches (hurls) the source away using Item::hurl.
notable_control_flow: U8P_FIRESHLD::gotHit branches on whether param1 refers to an NPC (Item::isNpc). NPCs use Npc::getDir to obtain direction; non‑NPCs use Item::getDirToItem.
The computed direction (local1) is compared against constants 0..7 and mapped to a pair of numeric values (local2, local3) which are passed to Item::hurl to control knockback/hurl behavior.
An impact sound is played unconditionally when a nonzero attacker/source is present (playSFX).

For most source item types the code calls METHOD::receiveHitWrapper with a small randomized magnitude (FREE::randomInRange) before hurling; some item types (checked via Item::getType against constants like 0x0105 and 0x0060) are exempted.

Before hurling the source, Kernel::getNumProcesses is consulted for a process type tied to the source; if present, FREE::resetRef is called to clear that reference, then Item::hurl is invoked and the process suspends/waits for the hurl to complete.
open_questions: Exact encoding/meaning of the numeric hurl parameters (local2 and local3) passed to Item::hurl and how each literal value affects motion/flags.
Concrete semantics and use cases for the second parameter of U8P_FIRESHLD::gotHit (param2) — the provided excerpt does not use it.
Precise item types represented by the numeric constants (e.g., 0x0105, 0x0060) and why those types are excluded from receiveHitWrapper.
Whether U8P_FIRESHLD::gotHit is invoked directly by the engine on collision events or by another external process (call site not present in this fragment).

main_function.name: U8P_FIRESHLD::look
main_function.purpose: Present the item's short description when the player inspects the Fire Shield.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player 'looks at' or examines the Fire Shield (object U8P_FIRESHLD::look/examine action).

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESHLD::look
function_map.role: Provide short description/inspect handler for the Fire Shield

function_map.name: U8P_FIRESHLD::gotHit
function_map.role: Handle collision/hit events: determine source direction, apply optional hit effect, and hurl the source away

function_map.name: Item::isNpc
function_map.role: Query whether a selector/id refers to an NPC (used to choose direction lookup)

function_map.name: Npc::getDir
function_map.role: Return NPC facing/direction (used to compute relative hit direction)

function_map.name: Item::getDirToItem
function_map.role: Compute direction from the shield to another item (used for non‑NPC sources and for receiveHitWrapper)

function_map.name: Item::getType
function_map.role: Return source item type (used to skip receiveHitWrapper for certain types)

function_map.name: METHOD::receiveHitWrapper
function_map.role: Apply a hit/damage effect with a magnitude and direction (invoked conditionally before hurl)

function_map.name: FREE::randomInRange
function_map.role: Produce a small random magnitude used with receiveHitWrapper

function_map.name: Kernel::getNumProcesses
function_map.role: Check for an existing process tied to the source (used to decide whether to reset a ref)

function_map.name: FREE::resetRef
function_map.role: Reset/clear a process reference for the source when needed

function_map.name: Item::hurl
function_map.role: Launch/hurl the source away from the shield using computed parameters

function_map.name: playSFX
function_map.role: Play impact sound when the shield is hit

external_calls.purpose: Decide whether param1 refers to an NPC so the script uses Npc::getDir vs Item::getDirToItem
Get NPC facing/direction (when source is an NPC) to compute knockback direction
Compute direction from the shield to a non‑NPC source; used for direction mapping and passed into receiveHitWrapper
Play an impact sound effect for the hit
Check source type and skip receiveHitWrapper for specific types
Apply a small randomized hit effect to target(s) before hurling
Generate the random magnitude passed to receiveHitWrapper
Check for a related process for the source so the script can reset references if necessary
Clear a stored process reference for the source before hurling it
Launch/hurl the source away using computed numeric parameters (local3, local2) and then suspend while the hurl/process runs

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the inspect ('U8P_FIRESHLD::look') description and a hit reaction for the Fire Shield item.
